content.text:
Recent top and latest posts on X matching your query "(AI observability OR agent monitoring OR LLM observability OR AI tracing OR agent safety) lang:en" focus on emerging tools, benchmarks, and risks for AI agents and LLMs. Here's a curated summary of key highlights from the past week:

### Breakthroughs in Agent Safety & Monitoring
- **AgentDoG**: A new state-of-the-art diagnostic guardrail framework for AI agents, diagnosing root causes like prompt injection and tool misuse with 82% accuracy—far better than general LLMs. Paper and code available.[[1]](https://x.com/i/status/2018980996528685464)[[2]](https://x.com/i/status/2019004087250067906)[[3]](https://x.com/i/status/2018985810792742923)
- **WoW (World of Workflows)**: New benchmark from Skyfall AI shows frontier LLMs failing at enterprise tasks due to "dynamic blindness"—unable to track action ripple effects against safety rules, posing compliance hazards.[[4]](https://x.com/i/status/2018368951697436753)[[5]](https://x.com/i/status/2018399156973506568)
- **OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Apps (2026)**: Covers real-world risks like permissions, injection, data exposure, and monitoring, with a practical safety checklist.[[6]](https://x.com/i/status/2019064453103260154)

### Observability & Tracing Tools
- **AgentSight**: Zero-instrumentation LLM agent observability using eBPF—no code changes needed for production insights into behavior.[[7]](https://x.com/i/status/2019151106224312796)
- **AgentBasis**: Emphasizes step-level traces for multi-step, tool-using agents to debug decisions, latency, costs, and failures beyond basic logs.[[8]](https://x.com/i/status/2018788027217465541)
- Calls for advanced metrics like invariance residue, saturation index, and irreversibility risk to detect structural failures in agents, beyond local traces.[[9]](https://x.com/i/status/2019074897880760340)

### Key Concerns & Debates
- Encrypting AI agent chat rooms (e.g., on AI-only platforms) hinders human visibility, debugging, accountability, and safety monitoring—seen as a "terrible idea."[[10]](https://x.com/i/status/2018082024607699276)
- Prompt injection risks in agentic setups: LLMs as supervisors can be bypassed; need independent verification and proof-based containment.[[11]](https://x.com/i/status/2019051165439144268)[[12]](https://x.com/i/status/2018688598858740070)
- Instrumentation poisoning: Autonomous agents as telemetry interfaces risk shaping developer perceptions if compromised.[[13]](https://x.com/i/status/2018224392354836653)

Prediction markets show low odds for major US AI safety bills soon (17% by 2025).[[14]](https://x.com/i/status/2018313196197622013) Overall, the conversation stresses proactive architectures, structural governance, and decentralized traceability for safe agent deployment.[[15]](https://x.com/i/status/2017734505163870586)
